title: Historical Hold Boundary Cadence Surface Host Portability Repair And Validation
historical_note: This record preserves one earlier Dev Residency execution slice from before the canonical repo boundary moved to C:/APEX Platform/apex-power-ops-platform on 2026-05-07. It is historical provenance, not a live host-portability repair surface for current repo operations.
current_routing: use PROJECT_STATUS.md for the current residue-retirement lane and latest completed packets
use docs/architecture/OLARES-PUBLICATION-BOUNDARY-RETIREMENT-DEPENDENCY-INVENTORY-2026-05-06.md for the remaining post-cutover boundary closeout queue
use this record only when historical provenance is needed for the earlier Dev Residency 057 host-portability repair slice preserved here
objective: Repair the new hold-boundary cadence surface so it runs on the authoritative Olares host mirror without extra Python dependencies, then rerun it there truthfully.
dependency_note: Packet 056 landed the cadence surface and validated it locally, but the first host rerun exposed a portability defect in the deferred-view helper.
execution_steps: Rerun the host wrapper from `/home/olares/code/apex/apex-power-ops-platform` and capture the portability failure.
Repair the helper to use the admitted `apex-db` MCP surface instead of host-local `sqlalchemy` imports.
Teach the minimal-trio wrappers to prefer `SEAM_DATABASE_URL` when a live DSN is intentionally supplied.
Revalidate locally, republish the small repair, fast-forward the host mirror, and rerun the host wrapper.
constraints: Do not install Python packages on the Olares host.
Do not widen AI-services admission beyond the admitted minimal MCP trio plus `apex-jobs`.
Do not treat a truthful `UNAVAILABLE` result as authority to reopen the deferred Operations Visibility seams.

repaired_host_result.decision: Authoritative deferred view counts require apex-db to run against a live DSN such as SEAM_DATABASE_URL; the current database surface is not sufficient for this hold check.

execution_rationale: The cadence surface was not complete while it only ran on the workstation posture and failed on the authoritative Olares mirror.
Using the admitted `apex-db` surface keeps the repair inside the current trust boundary instead of adding new host dependencies.
Preferring `SEAM_DATABASE_URL` in the trio wrappers preserves a truthful path to future live deferred-view checks without reopening broader AI or product scope.
next_packet_candidate: Rerun the cadence surface with `SEAM_DATABASE_URL` or another explicit live DSN only when a real deferred-view reopen decision is required, or reopen only a different bounded Olares lane with stronger evidence.
closure_note: Executed 2026-05-06 as the bounded follow-on to Packet 056. Packet 057 captured the initial host portability failure, repaired the helper by routing deferred-view checks through `apex-db`, republished the small fix in commit `038421cf13e52656ff6be8d9c767f74b9ef1e9fd`, and reran the host wrapper to a truthful `minimal_mcp=PASS`, `deferred_ops=UNAVAILABLE` result.
next_action: Keep the hold boundary closed until a live DSN is intentionally supplied for a real deferred-view recheck, or until a different bounded Olares lane presents stronger evidence.
